title: Warm Algerian Pistachio Tagine
description: A cozy, low‑glycemic tagine packed with tender chicken, chickpeas, and crunchy pistachios, finished in a fragrant banana leaf wrap.
instructions: Prepare a clean banana leaf and cut it into a square large enough to hold the tagine.
Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottom pot over medium heat and sear the chicken thighs until golden brown on all sides.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the pot and cook until the onion softens.
Stir in cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, sautéing for one minute to release their aromas.
Mix in the tomato paste and preserved lemon, cooking for another two minutes.
Pour in the water, add the cooked chickpeas, salt, and pepper, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat, cover, and let the tagine cook for 20 minutes to meld the flavors.
Fold in the shelled pistachios, stirring gently to distribute them evenly.
Transfer the hot tagine onto the banana leaf, cover the leaf with a lid or another leaf, and steam the dish for 15 minutes to finish cooking.
Uncover, fluff the mixture with a fork, and serve immediately with a side of whole‑grain bread or quinoa.
